---
title: "session2"
author: "jolynecujoh233"
date: "2026-03-09"
output: pdf_document
---

### The dataset

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# 设置R Markdown代码块的全局参数  Set global options for R Markdown code chunks
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, # 显示代码 / show code in output
                      warning = FALSE, # 不显示警告 / suppress warnings
                      message = FALSE, # 不显示package提示 / suppress messages
                      fig.width = 9,   # 图宽 / figure width
                      fig.height = 5)  # 图高 / figure height
options(max.print = 80) # 控制打印输出的最大行数
```


```{r packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggtext)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(scales)

# 设置默认绘图主题
theme_set(theme_minimal())

# 定义颜色调色板
pal <- c(
  nus_blue   = "#003D7C",
  blue       = "#2E75B6",
  nus_orange = "#EF7C00",
  ice        = "#D6E8F7",
  grey       = "#6B7D8C"
)
```

```{r install, eval=FALSE}
install.packages(c("ggtext", "patchwork", "ggrepel", "scales"))
```

```{r load-files}
# List all files in the data directory
files <- list.files("D:/2/Data/res", full.names = TRUE)
```

```{r read}
# Read all files and stack them into one data frame
residents_raw <- read_csv(files)
```


```{r load-hdb}
hdb <- read_csv("D:/2/data2/Resale flat prices based on registration date from Jan-2017 onwards.csv")
glimpse(hdb)
```

```{r clean-hdb}
hdb <- hdb %>%
  mutate(
    date      = ym(month),
    year_hdb  = year(date),
    price_psm = resale_price / floor_area_sqm,
    flat_type = str_to_title(flat_type),
    flat_type = fct_relevel(flat_type,
                  "1 Room", "2 Room", "3 Room", "4 Room",
                  "5 Room", "Executive", "Multi-Generation"),
    town      = str_to_title(town)
  )
hdb %>% count(flat_type)
```

### Colour palette and subsets

```{r hdb-subsets}
y_hdb <- max(hdb$year_hdb)

flat_colours <- c(
  "3 Room"    = "#1A3A5C",
  "4 Room"    = "#2E75B6",
  "5 Room"    = "#E8703A",
  "Executive" = "#0D8A8A"
)

# Plot A: annual median price by flat type
hdb_trend <- hdb %>%
  filter(flat_type %in% c("3 Room", "4 Room", "5 Room", "Executive")) %>%
  group_by(year_hdb, flat_type) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(resale_price), .groups = "drop")

# Plot B: most recent year's town medians for 4-Room flats
hdb_towns_4room <- hdb %>%
  filter(year_hdb == y_hdb, flat_type == "4 Room") %>%
  group_by(town) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(resale_price),
            n            = n(),
            .groups      = "drop") %>%
  filter(n >= 30)
```


```{r hdb-check1}
glimpse(hdb_trend)
```
```{r hdb-check2}
glimpse(hdb_towns_4room)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 1 — The baseline: where we start

```{r baseline-a}
p_A <- ggplot(hdb_trend, aes(x = year_hdb, y = median_price, colour = flat_type)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = flat_colours)
p_A
```

```{r baseline-b}
p_B <- ggplot(hdb_towns_4room,
              aes(x = median_price, y = reorder(town, median_price))) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue")
p_B
```


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 2 — patchwork: composing figures


```{r patchwork-sidebyside, fig.height=4}
p_A + p_B        # side by side
```

```{r patchwork-stacked, fig.height=8}
p_A / p_B        # stacked
```


```{r patchwork-annotation, fig.height=8}
(p_A / p_B) +
  plot_annotation(
    title      = "HDB Resale Flat Prices — Two perspectives",
    subtitle   = paste0("A - All flats, 2017-", y_hdb, " | B - 4-Room flats: ", y_hdb, " only"),
    caption    = "Source: HDB, data.gov.sg",
    tag_levels = "A"
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Level 3 — ggrepel and plot-type redesign

### Alternative for the bar chart for Plot B


```{r dot-step1}
ggplot(hdb_towns_4room,
       aes(x = median_price, y = reorder(town, median_price))) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = min(median_price) * 0.92,
                   xend = median_price, yend = town),
               colour = "grey80") +
  geom_point(colour = pal["nus_blue"], size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format(prefix = "S$", big.mark = ",")) +
  labs(title = paste0("Median 4-Room resale price by town, ", y_hdb),
       x = "Median resale price (S$)", y = NULL,
       caption = "Source: HDB, data.gov.sg. Towns with >= 30 transactions.")
```

### Adding ggrepel: labelling the expensive outliers


```{r dot-with-repel, fig.height=6}
ggplot(hdb_towns_4room,
       aes(x = median_price,
           y = reorder(town, median_price),
           label = if_else(median_price > 620000, town, NA_character_))) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = min(median_price) * 0.92,
                   xend = median_price, yend = town),
               colour = "grey80") +
  geom_point(aes(colour = median_price > 620000), size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(
    nudge_x      = 15000,
    direction    = "y",
    size         = 3.0,
    colour       = pal["nus_orange"],
    segment.size = 0.3,
    na.rm        = TRUE,
    seed         = 42
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("TRUE" = pal["nus_orange"], "FALSE" = pal["blue"]),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format(prefix = "S$", big.mark = ",")) +
  labs(title = "Which towns command the highest 4-Room prices?",
       subtitle = "Orange: towns with median above S$620,000",
       x = "Median resale price (S$)", y = NULL,
       caption = "Source: HDB, data.gov.sg. Towns with >= 30 transactions.")
```

```{r final-composed, fig.height=10}
p_B <- ggplot(hdb_towns_4room,
              aes(x = median_price,
                  y = reorder(town, median_price),
                  label = if_else(median_price > 620000, town, NA_character_))) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = min(median_price) * 0.92,
                   xend = median_price, yend = town),
               colour = "grey80") +
  geom_point(aes(colour = median_price > 620000), size = 3) +
  geom_text_repel(nudge_x = 15000, direction = "y", size = 3.0,
                  colour = pal["nus_orange"], segment.size = 0.3,
                  na.rm = TRUE, seed = 42) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("TRUE" = pal["nus_orange"], "FALSE" = pal["nus_blue"]),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format(prefix = "S$", big.mark = ",")) +
  labs(x = "Median resale price (S$)", y = NULL)

(p_A / p_B) +
  plot_annotation(
    title      = "Singapore HDB resale market: Post-pandemic surge through two perspectives",
    caption    = "Source: HDB, data.gov.sg",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", colour = pal["nus_blue"]),
      plot.caption = element_text(colour = "grey60", size = 9)
    )
  )
```
